message:
fix(update-check,bd-87sqx): allow-list scheme + host for CASS_UPDATE_API_BASE_URL override

Pre-fix: src/update_check.rs::release_api_base_url returned whatever
CASS_UPDATE_API_BASE_URL was set to with zero validation. A malicious
.env file or polluted shell rc could redirect the release-metadata
fetch to an attacker-controlled endpoint. Impact was bounded (the
install-script URL at run_self_update is hardcoded + https + version-
validated, so no RCE redirect) but spoofed release JSON could phish
the update prompt and downgrade to plaintext http://.

Fix: validate every override against an allow-list.
- `https://<host>...` — any non-empty non-root-only host accepted.
- `http://127.0.0.1:*`, `http://localhost:*`, `http://[::1]:*` —
  loopback-only plaintext allowed for the existing integration-test
  pattern (rustup, cargo, npm all treat localhost as a trusted
  MitM-free zone). Exact host match, no prefix-extension attacks
  (e.g., `http://127.0.0.1.attacker.com` rejected).
- Anything else → one-shot stderr warning, fall through to the
  default `https://api.github.com/repos/{GITHUB_REPO}`.

Pure-function helper `is_allowed_update_api_url(url)` factored out so
the four new unit tests can pin accept/reject cases independently of
env-var plumbing:
- test_is_allowed_update_api_url_allows_https_any_host
- test_is_allowed_update_api_url_allows_http_loopback_only
- test_is_allowed_update_api_url_rejects_non_loopback_http (including
  prefix-extension attack vectors)
- test_is_allowed_update_api_url_rejects_other_schemes (ftp, file,
  gopher, empty, bare-host, empty-host-https)

IPv6 `[::1]` bracket form handled separately from IPv4/hostname path
since a naive `.find(':')` would split inside the brackets.

Six existing integration tests continue to pass unchanged — all use
`http://127.0.0.1:<ephemeral-port>`, which the loopback allow-list
admits.

Validation: 31/31 update_check unit tests pass; cargo fmt --check clean.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-87sqx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
